briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handlers. On initialization, All handlers are set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is the type of the interrupts to enable. Use OR'ing of XZDMA_IXR_DMA_*_MASK constants defined in xzdma_hw.h to create this parameter value.

briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the DMA done callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handlers. On initialization, Done handler are set to this callback.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This static function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in simple mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.
is a pointer of array to the XZDma_Transfer structure which has all the configuration fields for initiating data transfer.

briefdescription.para: This static function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in Linear descriptor type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.
is a pointer of array to the XZDma_Transfer structure which has all the configuration fields for initiating data transfer.
is descriptor pointer of source in which Data fields has to be filled.
is descriptor pointer of destination in which Data fields has to be filled.

specifies whether provided descriptor pointer is last one or not.
XZDMA_TRUE - If descriptor is last
XZDMA_FALSE - If descriptor is not last

memberdef.argsstring: (XZDma_LiDscr *DscrPtr, u64 Addr, u32 Size, u32 CtrlValue)
memberdef.name: XZDma_ConfigLinear

briefdescription.para: This static function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in Linear descriptor type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to source/destination descriptor.
is a 64 bit variable which denotes the address of data.
specifies the amount of the data to be transferred.
contains all the control fields of descriptor.

briefdescription.para: This static function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in Linked list descriptor type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.
is a pointer of array to the XZDma_Transfer structure which has all the configuration fields for initiating data transfer.
is descriptor pointer of source in which Data fields has to be filled.
is descriptor pointer of destination in which Data fields has to be filled.

specifies whether provided descriptor pointer is last one or not.
TRUE - If descriptor is last
FALSE - If descriptor is not last

memberdef.argsstring: (XZDma_LlDscr *DscrPtr, u64 Addr, u32 Size, u32 CtrlValue, u64 NextDscrAddr)
memberdef.name: XZDma_ConfigLinkedList

briefdescription.para: This static function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in Linked list descriptor type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to source/destination descriptor.
is a 64 bit variable which denotes the address of data.
specifies the amount of the data to be transferred.
contains all the control fields of descriptor.
is the address of next descriptor.

briefdescription.para: This static function gets all the reset configurations of ZDMA.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes an ZDMA core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using an ZDMA core. Initialization of an ZDMA includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state and resets all the hardware configurations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XZDma instance.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, pass in the physical address instead.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the pointer type and mode in which ZDMA needs to transfer the data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a variable which specifies whether transfer has to to be done in scatter gather mode or simple mode.
TRUE - Scatter gather pointer type
FALSE - Simple pointer type

is the type of the mode in which data has to be initiated
XZDMA_NORMAL_MODE - Normal data transfer from source to destination (Valid for both Scatter gather and simple types)
XZDMA_WRONLY_MODE - Write only mode (Valid only for Simple)
XZDMA_RDONLY_MODE - Read only mode (Valid only for Simple)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If mode has been set successfully.
XST_FAILURE - If mode has not been set.

simplesect.para: Mode cannot be changed while ZDMA is not in IDLE state.

memberdef.argsstring: (XZDma *InstancePtr, XZDma_DscrType TypeOfDscr, UINTPTR Dscr_MemPtr, u32 NoOfBytes)
memberdef.name: XZDma_CreateBDList

briefdescription.para: This function sets the descriptor type and descriptor pointer's start address of both source and destination based on the memory allocated by user and also calculates no of descriptors(BDs) can be created in the allocated memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a variable which specifies descriptor type whether Linear or linked list type of descriptor.
XZDMA_LINEAR - Linear type of descriptor.
XZDMA_LINKEDLIST- Linked list type of descriptor.
is a pointer to the allocated memory for creating descriptors. It Should be aligned to 64 bytes.
specifies the number of bytes allocated for descriptors

simplesect.para: The Count of the descriptors can be created.

User should allocate the memory for descriptors which should be capable of how many transfers he wish to do in one start. For Linear mode each descriptor needs 128 bit memory so for one data transfer it requires 2*128 = 256 bits i.e. 32 bytes Similarly for Linked list mode for each descriptor it needs 256 bit, so for one data transfer it require 2*256 = 512 bits i.e. 64 bytes.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the data attributes and control configurations of a ZDMA core based on the inputs provided.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer to the XZDma_ChDataConfig structure which has all the configuration fields. The fields of the structure are:
OverFetch - Allows over fetch or not
0 - Not allowed to over-fetch on SRC
1 - Allowed to over-fetch on SRC
SrcIssue - Outstanding transaction on SRC
Range is 1 to 32
SrcBurstType - Burst Type for SRC AXI transaction
XZDMA_FIXED_BURST - Fixed burst
XZDMA_INCR_BURST - Incremental burst
SrcBurstLen - AXI Length for Data Read.
Range of values is (1,2,4,8,16).
DstBurstType - Burst Type for SRC AXI transaction
XZDMA_FIXED_BURST - Fixed burst
XZDMA_INCR_BURST - Incremental burst
DstBurstLen - AXI Length for Data write.
Range of values is (1,2,4,8,16).
SrcCache - AXI cache bits for Data read
SrcQos - Configurable QoS bits for AXI Data read
DstCache - AXI cache bits for Data write
DstQos - configurable QoS bits for AXI Data write

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE If ZDMA Core is not in Idle state and
XST_SUCCESS If Configurations are made successfully
These configurations will last till we modify or Reset by XZDma_Reset(XZDma *InstancePtr).
Configurations should be modified only when ZDMA channel is IDLE this can be confirmed by using XZDma_ChannelState(XZDma *InstancePtr) API.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the data attributes and control configurations of a ZDMA core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer to the XZDma_ChDataConfig structure which has all the configuration fields. The fields of the structure are:
OverFetch - Allows over fetch or not
0 - Not allowed to over-fetch on SRC
1 - Allowed to over-fetch on SRC
SrcIssue - Outstanding transaction on SRC
Range is 1 to 32
SrcBurstType - Burst Type for SRC AXI transaction
XZDMA_FIXED_BURST - Fixed burst
XZDMA_INCR_BURST - Incremental burst
SrcBurstLen - AXI Length for Data Read.
Can be max of 16 to be compatible with AXI3
DstBurstType - Burst Type for SRC AXI transaction
XZDMA_FIXED_BURST - Fixed burst
XZDMA_INCR_BURST - Incremental burst
DstBurstLen - AXI Length for Data write.
Can be max of 16 to be compatible with AXI3
SrcCache - AXI cache bits for Data read
SrcQos - Configurable QoS bits for AXI Data read
DstCache - AXI cache bits for Data write
DstQos - Configurable QoS bits for AXI Data write

briefdescription.para: This function sets the descriptor attributes based on the inputs provided in the structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer to the XZDma_ChDscrConfig structure which has all the configuration fields. The fields of the structure are:
AxCoherent - AXI transactions generated for the descriptor.
0 - Non coherent
1 - Coherent
AXCache - AXI cache bit used for DSCR fetch (both on SRC and DST Side)
AXQos - QoS bit used for DSCR fetch (both on SRC and DST Side)

listitem.para:
XST_FAILURE If ZDMA core is not in Idle state and
XST_SUCCESS If Configurations are made successfully

briefdescription.para: This function gets the descriptor attributes of the channel.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer to the XZDma_ChDscrConfig structure which has all the configuration fields. The fields of the structure are:
AxCoherent - AXI transactions generated for the descriptor.
0 - Non coherent
1 - Coherent
AXCache - AXI cache bit used for DSCR fetch (both on SRC and DST Side)
AXQos - QoS bit used for DSCR fetch (both on SRC and DST Side)

briefdescription.para: This function preloads the buffers which will be used in write only mode.

detaileddescription.para: In write only mode the data in the provided buffer will be written in destination address for specified size.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer to an array of 64/128 bit data. i.e. pointer to 32 bit array of size 2/4
Array of Size 2 for ADMA
Array of Size 4 for GDMA

simplesect.para: Valid only in simple mode. Prior to call this function ZDMA instance should be set in Write only mode by using XZDma_SetMode(XZDma *InstancePtr, u8 IsSgDma, XZDma_Mode Mode) To initiate data transfer after this API need to call XZDma_Start(XZDma *InstancePtr, XZDma_Transfer *Data, u32 Num) In which only destination fields has to be filled.

briefdescription.para: This function resume the paused state of ZDMA core and starts the transfer from where it has paused.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

simplesect.para: Valid only for scatter gather mode.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the ZDMA core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

simplesect.para: This function resets all the configurations made previously. Disables all the interrupts and clears interrupt status.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the state of ZDMA core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

simplesect.para:
This function returns state of ZDMA core
XZDMA_IDLE - If ZDMA core is in idle state.
XZDMA_PAUSE - If ZDMA is in paused state.
XZDMA_BUSY - If ZDMA is in busy state.

briefdescription.para: This function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer.

detaileddescription.para: Data transfer elements needs to be passed through structure pointer. Data transfer can be done in any of the three modes (simple, Linear or Linked List) based on the selected mode but before calling this API make sure that ZDMA is in Idle state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

is a pointer of array to the XZDma_Transfer structure which has all the configuration fields for initiating data transfer. The fields of the structure are:
SrcAddr - Source address
DstAddr - Destination address
Size - size of the data to be transferred in bytes
SrcCoherent - AXI transactions generated to process the descriptor payload for source channel
0 - Non coherent
1 - Coherent
DstCoherent - AXI transactions generated to process the descriptor payload for destination channel
0 - Non coherent
1 - Coherent
Pause - Valid only for scatter gather mode. Will pause after completion of this descriptor.

specifies number of array elements of Data pointer.
For simple mode Num should be equal to 1
For Scatter gather mode (either linear or linked list) Num can be any choice. (But based on which memory should be allocated by Application) It should be less than the return value of XZDma_CreateBDList.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - if ZDMA initiated the transfer.
XST_FAILURE - if ZDMA has not initiated data transfer.

simplesect.para:
After Pause to resume the transfer need to use the following API
XZDma_Resume User should provide allocated memory and descriptor type in scatter gather mode through the following API before calling the start API.
XZDma_SetDescriptorType(XZDma *InstancePtr, XZDma_DscrType TypeOfDscr, UINTPTR Dscr_MemPtr, u32 NoOfBytes)

briefdescription.para: This function sets all the required fields for initiating data transfer in scatter gather mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.
is a pointer of array to the XZDma_Transfer structure which has all the configuration fields for initiating data transfer.
specifies number of array elements of Data pointer.

briefdescription.para: This function enables all the interrupts which user intended to enable and enables the ZDMA channel for initiating data transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

briefdescription.para: Stops the ZDMA engine and releases power management resources if supported.

detaileddescription.para: This function asserts the validity of the input instance pointer. If power management (XPM_SUPPORT) is enabled, it releases the power management node associated with the ZDMA instance. If the release fails, an error message is printed. The function returns the status of the operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XZDma instance.

simplesect.para:
Status code:
XST_SUCCESS if successful.
Error code if PM resource release fails (when XPM_SUPPORT is defined).
